tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Using the provided MRI image, determine the anatomic location of the indicated mass (white arrow).

dialogs.thought: First orient to modality/plane and identify where the arrow points relative to spine and mediastinum.

content.content: Axial MRI of the thorax at an upper thoracic level. The vertebral body and spinal canal are central/posterior, with lungs laterally. A white arrow points to a soft-tissue mass adjacent to the vertebral body in the posterior mediastinum/paraspinal region.

dialogs.thought: Check for any on-image labels indicating vertebral level or orientation (R/L) to localize the lesion level.

arguments.attribute: Describe the lesion indicated by the arrow and its relationship to the vertebral body/spinal canal and mediastinum.

dialogs.thought: Confirm the arrowed structure is a posterior mediastinal/paraspinal mass rather than intrapulmonary or spinal canal lesion.

content.content: Within the boxed region there is a rounded/ovoid soft-tissue mass in the paraspinal/posterior mediastinal space, abutting the lateral aspect of the vertebral body. It is outside the spinal canal and separate from the lung parenchyma.

dialogs.content: The mass is located in the posterior mediastinum at the T4-5 level of the thoracic spine.

whitelist: The mass is located in the posterior mediastinum at the T4-5 level of the thoracic spine.
